steps.description: Click the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown to start creating a new declarative agent within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'Add action' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top center of the application window.
Select "Add Action" from the command palette dropdown in Visual Studio Code by clicking on the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Action" option highlighted below the search box.
Click on the "Start with an OpenAPI Description Document" option in the "Add an Action" dropdown menu within Visual Studio Code’s M365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Click the text input field labeled "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location or Open File..." in the "OpenAPI Spec Document" pop-up within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document Location" option in the dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code window, within the "OpenAPI Description Document" input dialog.

Type the URL 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/huimiu/api-spec-example/main/repair-service.yml' into the "Enter OpenAPI Description Document URL" input field in the pop-up dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Press Enter to confirm the entered OpenAPI Description Document URL in the input dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the blue "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" dialog to confirm the selected operations in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "OK" button in the "Select Operation(s) Copilot Can Interact with" modal dialog to confirm operation selection in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dropdown within the "Select Teams manifest_json File" dialog in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Proceed" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code.
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/ai-plugin.json exist. Timeout: 10s
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/apiSpecificationFile/openapi.yaml exist. Timeout: 10s
Press Ctrl+P to open the Quick Open file search in  the  Visual Studio Code
Type 'declarativeAgent.json' into the search input field of the file quick open bar at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.
Click the "declarativeAgent.json" file entry in the VS Code quick open bar to select and open the file.
@assertion  The actions attribute exists and includes a id\"action_1\"  in  the declarativeAgent.json file.
Click the "Close (Ctrl+W)" button on the tab for "declarativeAgent.json" in the Visual Studio Code editor to close the file.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'provision' into the VS Code Command Palette input field, located at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "Provision" option under "Microsoft 365 Agents" in the VS Code command palette dropdown to initiate the provisioning process.
Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type 'notifications:show notifications' into the VS Code command palette input field at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the VS Code command palette dropdown to select it.
@code wait for #text:/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}/appPackage/build/manifest.dev.json exist. Timeout: 20s
@assertion notification \"provision stage executed successfully\" exist.
